• All book proceeds benefit EFF's digital rights advocacy work
• Events focus on data security, federal data access, and digital rights

items.title: US Govt Rewrites Procurement Rules to Force AI Companies to Disable Safety Guardrails
items.summary: GSA proposes procurement rules requiring AI contractors to license systems for 'all lawful purposes' and disable safety guardrails that might refuse government requests, effectively weaponizing contracts against AI safety.
items.content_text:
General Services Administration proposes new procurement rules targeting AI contractors
• Requirements include licensing AI systems to government for 'all lawful purposes'
• Companies must disable safety guardrails that could refuse government requests
• Rules emerge amid DOD-Anthropic dispute over AI use for mass surveillance
• EFF and coalition filed comments opposing rules as threat to AI safety
• Proposal includes 'anti-Woke' requirements described as technologically incoherent
• Rules would become standard components of every federal contract if adopted
• Government using procurement power to circumvent direct regulatory resistance

items.title: Nicole Ozer Named New EFF Executive Director, Bringing Anti-Surveillance Expertise
items.summary: Nicole Ozer, architect of California's strongest electronic surveillance laws, will lead EFF starting June 1, succeeding Cindy Cohn after 25+ years with the organization.
items.content_text:
Nicole Ozer appointed as EFF's new executive director effective June 1, 2025
• Succeeds Cindy Cohn, who led EFF for nearly a decade and worked there 25+ years
• Ozer previously founding director of ACLU Northern California's Technology and Civil Liberties Program (2004-2025)
• Led passage of California Electronic Communications Privacy Act - nation's strongest warrant requirement for electronic data
• Created model surveillance oversight law adopted in 25+ jurisdictions protecting 17+ million people
• Developed campaigns against social media surveillance and facial recognition at major tech companies
• Brings two decades of litigation experience including Supreme Court cases
• Transition comes as EFF celebrates 35th anniversary and faces 'critical times' for digital rights

items.title: Durov: Thousands of Developers Building VPNs to Counter State Internet Control
items.summary: Telegram CEO Pavel Durov reports thousands of software developers are actively creating VPNs to circumvent government internet restrictions.
items.content_text:
Pavel Durov states thousands of developers building VPNs for censorship circumvention
• Development activity appears to be response to state internet control measures
• Iran context suggests connection to recent internet restrictions and protests
• Represents distributed effort rather than centralized anti-censorship development
• Indicates growing technical resistance to government internet controls
• Durov's platform gives him visibility into global circumvention tool usage

items.title: Press Freedom Coalition Challenges Trump's Executive Order Targeting Law Firms
items.summary: 42 media organizations led by The Intercept filed amicus brief challenging Trump's executive order sanctioning law firms that represent his political opponents, arguing it undermines press freedom.
items.content_text:
The Intercept's Press Freedom Defense Fund leads coalition of 42 media organizations in legal challenge
• Trump executive order imposed sanctions on law firms representing his political opponents
• Four lower district courts already ruled the executive order unconstitutional
• Coalition filed amicus brief with U.S. Court of Appeals for D.C. Circuit to affirm lower court rulings
• Argument centers on executive order restricting press access to legal counsel, especially pro bono representation
• Coalition includes news organizations, press associations, and First Amendment attorneys with 500+ years collective experience
• Legal challenge positions independent legal counsel as essential infrastructure for press freedom

items.title: Judge Blocks DOD Blacklisting of Anthropic as First Amendment Retaliation
items.summary: Federal judge granted preliminary injunction blocking Department of War's blacklisting of Anthropic, ruling it was unconstitutional retaliation for the company's critical press statements.
items.content_text:
US District Judge Rita Lin blocked Department of War's blacklisting of Anthropic AI company
• Judge ruled the action constituted 'classic First Amendment retaliation'
• DOD designated Anthropic as supply-chain risk due to 'hostile manner through the press'
• Court found no evidence Anthropic posed urgent national security threat
• Officials failed to consider less restrictive alternatives before extreme measures
• Preliminary injunction granted, suggesting Anthropic likely to prevail on merits
• Sets precedent against government retaliation targeting AI companies for speech
